1.q: The three transgenic OE-17, OE-18, and OE-19 lines had significantly improved photosynthetic rates in the leaves in response to salt stress compared to the wild-type.

2.q: high salinity treatment reduced the concentrations of MDA, H2O2 and proline but elevated the activities of APX, GPX, CAT and SOD in the TsApx6-overexpressing plants.

3.q: TsHKT-based RNA interference in T. salsuginea resulted in Na+ sensitivity and K+ deficiency.

4.q: SOS1 (for Salt Overly Sensitive 1), encoding a sodium/proton antiporter, contribution to plant salinity tolerance; thsos1-RNAi lines showed faster Na+ accumulation and apoplastic sodium influx into the stele.

5.q: TsTIP1;2 was not permeable to Na+ in Xenopus oocytes, but it could facilitate the entry of Na+ ions into plant cell vacuoles by an indirect process under high-salinity conditions.
